title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: Netty: HAProxy SSL TLV parsing leaks retained slice on invalid TLV length
description: When decoding a PP2_TYPE_SSL TLV, HAProxyMessage.readNextTLV() first calls `header.retainedSlice(header.readerIndex(), length)` and only then reads the 1-byte client field and 4-byte verify field. If the attacker sets the TLV length below 5, the subsequent readByte/readInt throws IndexOutOfBoundsException. HAProxyMessageDecoder only catches HAProxyProtocolException around this call, so the IOOBE propagates and the retained slice on the pooled cumulation buffer is never released.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in Netty's HAProxy SSL TLV parsing can lead to memory leaks due to unreleased retained slices in pooled buffers. An attacker could exploit this issue to cause resource exhaustion, leading to potential denial-of-service (DoS) conditions in affected systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Netty that addresses CVE-2026-44893 as soon as it becomes available.
Implement input validation and length checks for incoming HAProxy messages to mitigate potential exploitation.
Monitor system resource usage for signs of memory leaks or unusual behavior and establish alerts for potential DoS conditions.

title: Netty has a Vulnerable Default Configuration Which Leads to Denial of Service via Unbounded HTTP/3 Header Size
description:
### Summary
The default configuration of the `Http3ConnectionHandler` in the Netty HTTP/3 codec lacks an enforced maximum header size limit. When a peer does not explicitly specify `HTTP3_SETTINGS_MAX_FIELD_SECTION_SIZE`, the implementation defaults to an unbounded limit. This insecure default configuration allows a malicious client or server to send an enormous number of headers, leading to a memory exhaustion Denial of Service via an `OutOfMemoryError`.

### Details
Netty securely limits heade

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44892.

title: Netty has Unbounded Direct Memory Consumption in its RedisDecoder
description:
### Summary
An attacker can cause DoS by sending crafted Redis payloads across multiple connections without `\r\n`. This exhausts the server's direct memory pool (OutOfDirectMemoryError), preventing legitimate connections from being processed.

### Details
io.netty.handler.codec.redis.RedisDecoder decodes the length of bulk strings and array headers using the `decodeLength` method. This method reads bytes from the network until it encounters a `\n` character. However, it does not enforce any max

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Netty's RedisDecoder allows an attacker to exhaust the server's direct memory pool by sending crafted Redis payloads, leading to a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. This can disrupt legitimate operations and render the affected server unresponsive.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Netty that addresses CVE-2026-44890 to ensure the vulnerability is mitigated.
Implement network-level protections such as rate limiting and traffic filtering to detect and block malicious payloads.
Monitor server memory usage and set up alerts for abnormal spikes in direct memory consumption.

title: Netty: Memory Exhaustion in RedisArrayAggregator due to Deeply Nested Arrays
description:
### Summary
An attacker can cause DoS by sending a crafted Redis payload with deeply nested arrays. This forces the server to allocate a massive number of state objects and collections, leading to memory exhaustion and an OutOfMemoryError.

### Details
io.netty.handler.codec.redis.RedisArrayAggregator aggregates RedisMessage parts into ArrayRedisMessage. It uses a `Deque<AggregateState>` to keep track of nested arrays. However, it does not limit the maximum depth of nested arrays. When an attack

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) by exploiting the RedisArrayAggregator in Netty. By sending crafted Redis payloads with deeply nested arrays, servers can experience memory exhaustion and crash due to OutOfMemoryError, potentially disrupting services and operations.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest patch or update for Netty that addresses CVE-2026-44250 to limit the maximum depth of nested arrays in RedisArrayAggregator.
Implement input validation and rate limiting to detect and block malicious payloads with deeply nested arrays.
Monitor server memory usage and set alerts for unusual spikes to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: Netty has an IPv6 Subnet Filter Bypass via Incorrect Comparator Masking
description:
### Summary
An attacker can bypass IPv6 subnet rules due to an incorrect masking operation in IpSubnetFilterRule.compareTo(). Valid public IP addresses can bypass the restrictions.

### Details
`io.netty.handler.ipfilter.IpSubnetFilterRule#compareTo(java.net.InetSocketAddress)` method performs a bitwise AND between the incoming IP address and the configured networkAddress, instead of the subnetMask.

### Impact
Access Control Bypass. Attacker can bypass IpSubnetFilter IPv6 access controls.

title: A vulnerability was identified in D-Link DGS-1100-08PD 1.00.006. This issue affects some unknown...
description: A vulnerability was identified in D-Link DGS-1100-08PD 1.00.006. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file /etc/boa.conf of the component Web Interface. Such manipulation leads to least privilege violation. The attack may be launched remotely. The attack requires a high level of complexity. The exploitability is assessed as difficult. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in D-Link DGS-1100-08PD 1.00.006 could allow unauthorized access or manipulation of the web interface configuration file, potentially leading to a least privilege violation. However, due to the high complexity required for exploitation and its low severity rating, the overall impact is limited.
recommended_actions: Update the firmware of the affected device to the latest version if available.
Restrict remote access to the web interface and ensure strong authentication mechanisms are in place.
Monitor for any unusual activity or signs of exploitation, especially if the device is exposed to the internet.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda F451 1.0.0.7/1.0.0.9. Impacted is the function...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda F451 1.0.0.7/1.0.0.9. Impacted is the function formWriteFacMac of the file /goform/WriteFacMac of the component Web Management Interface. Performing a manipulation of the argument mac results in os command injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Tenda F451 routers allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands via the Web Management Interface. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, network compromise, or the use of affected devices in botnet operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply firmware updates or patches provided by Tenda, if available.
Restrict access to the Web Management Interface by disabling remote management or using a VPN for secure access.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

This manipulation causes least privilege violation. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in TOTOLINK CP450 4.1.0cu.747 allows remote attackers to exploit the vsftpd configuration file, potentially leading to a least privilege violation. This could result in unauthorized access or privilege escalation, though the severity is categorized as low due to limited impact and scope.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available firmware updates or patches from TOTOLINK to address the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected device by configuring firewall rules to limit exposure to trusted IP ranges.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS) to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drm/amdgpu/userq: fix access...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drm/amdgpu/userq: fix access to stale wptr mapping

Use drm_exec to take both locks i.e vm root bo and
wptr_obj bo to access the mapping data properly.

This fixes the security issue of unmap the wptr_obj while
a queue creation is in progress and passing other
bo at same address.

(cherry picked from commit 1fc6c8ab45dbee096469c08c13f6099d57a52d6c)

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46311.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: videobuf2: Set...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: videobuf2: Set vma_flags in vb2_dma_sg_mmap

vb2_dma_contig sets VMA flags VM_DONTEXPAND and VM_DONTDUMP and I do not
see a reason why vb2_dma_sg should behave differently. This avoids
hitting `WARN_ON(!(vma->vm_flags & VM_DONTEXPAND));` in
drm_gem_mmap_obj() during mmap() of an imported dma-buf from the out of
tree Apple ISP camera capture driver which uses vb2_dma_sg_memops.

gst-launch-1.0 v4l2src ! gtk4paintablesink

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drm/v3d: Reject empty...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drm/v3d: Reject empty multisync extension to prevent infinite loop

v3d_get_extensions() walks a userspace-provided singly-linked list of
ioctl extensions without any bound on the chain length. A local user
can craft a self-referential extension (ext->next == &ext) with zero
in_sync_count and out_sync_count, which bypasses the existing duplicate-
extension guard:

    if (se->in_sync_count || se->out_sync_count)
            ret

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46314.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: renesas: vsp1: Fix...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: renesas: vsp1: Fix NULL pointer deref on module unload

When unloading the module on gen 4, we hit a NULL pointer dereference.
This is caused by the cleanup code calling vsp1_drm_cleanup() where it
should be calling vsp1_vspx_cleanup().

Fix this by checking the IP version and calling the drm or vspx function
accordingly, the same way as the init code does.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46310.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: intel/ipu6: fix error...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: intel/ipu6: fix error pointer dereference

In a error path isp->psys is confirmed to be an error pointer not NULL so
this condition is true and the error pointer is dereferenced. So isp-psys
should be set to NULL before going to out_ipu6_bus_del_devices.

Detected by Smatch:
drivers/media/pci/intel/ipu6/ipu6.c:690 ipu6_pci_probe() error:
'isp->psys' dereferencing possible ERR_PTR()

[Sakari Ailus: Fix commit message.]

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability involves an error pointer dereference in the Intel IPU6 driver within the Linux kernel. Exploitation could potentially lead to system crashes, denial of service, or unexpected behavior in systems using affected drivers.
recommended_actions: Update Linux kernel to the latest version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-46313.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using Intel IPU6 drivers to ensure compatibility with the updated kernel.
Monitor vendor advisories and apply additional patches or mitigations as needed.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

isofs: validate Rock Ridge...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

isofs: validate Rock Ridge CE continuation extent against volume size

rock_continue() reads rs->cont_extent verbatim from the Rock Ridge CE
record and passes it to sb_bread() without checking that the block
number is within the mounted ISO 9660 volume.  commit e595447e177b
("[PATCH] rock.c: handle corrupted directories") added cont_offset
and cont_size rejection for the CE continuation but did not validate
the extent block num

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46303.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drm/xe/uapi: Reject coh_none...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drm/xe/uapi: Reject coh_none PAT index for CPU cached memory in madvise

Add validation in xe_vm_madvise_ioctl() to reject PAT indices with
XE_COH_NONE coherency mode when applied to CPU cached memory.

Using coh_none with CPU cached buffers is a security issue. When the
kernel clears pages before reallocation, the clear operation stays in
CPU cache (dirty). GPU with coh_none can bypass CPU caches and read
stale sensitive data

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a GPU with coh_none mode to bypass CPU caches and access stale sensitive data, potentially leading to unauthorized data exposure or leakage. This could impact systems that rely on the Linux kernel for secure memory management.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-46309 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit systems to identify and address any configurations that may be using coh_none with CPU cached memory.
Implement monitoring and logging to detect unusual GPU memory access patterns that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: ath5k: do not access...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: ath5k: do not access array OOB

Vincent reports:
> The ath5k driver seems to do an array-index-out-of-bounds access as
> shown by the UBSAN kernel message:
> UBSAN: array-index-out-of-bounds in drivers/net/wireless/ath/ath5k/base.c:1741:20
> index 4 is out of range for type 'ieee80211_tx_rate [4]'
> ...
> Call Trace:
>  <TASK>
>  dump_stack_lvl+0x5d/0x80
>  ubsan_epilogue+0x5/0x2b
>  __ubsan_handle_out_of_bounds.cold+0x46

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's ath5k WiFi driver could allow an attacker to exploit an out-of-bounds array access, potentially leading to kernel crashes, denial of service, or arbitrary code execution. This could disrupt systems relying on affected WiFi hardware.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-46307.
Audit systems using the ath5k driver to identify vulnerable devices and prioritize patching.
Implement network segmentation and monitoring to detect and mitigate potential exploitation attempts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

flow_dissector: do not...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

flow_dissector: do not dissect PPPoE PFC frames

RFC 2516 Section 7 states that Protocol Field Compression (PFC) is NOT
RECOMMENDED for PPPoE. In practice, pppd does not support negotiating
PFC for PPPoE sessions, and the flow dissector driver has assumed an
uncompressed frame until the blamed commit.

During the review process of that commit [1], support for PFC is
suggested. However, having a compressed (1-byte) protocol fiel

title: CVE-2026-10216 - A vulnerability was detected in unitedbyai droidclaw up to 0.5.3. The affected element is an unknown
description: A vulnerability was detected in unitedbyai droidclaw up to 0.5.3. The affected element is an unknown function of the file server/src/routes/pairing.ts of the component claim Endpoint. The manipulation results in improper restriction of excessive authentication attempts. The attack may be launched remotely. This attack is characterized by high complexity. The exploitability is described as difficult. The exploit is now public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

title: CVE-2026-10215 - A security vulnerability has been detected in Dolibarr ERP CRM up to 23.0.1. Impacted is the functio
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Dolibarr ERP CRM up to 23.0.1. Impacted is the function checkUserAccessToObject of the file htdocs/holiday/class/api_holidays.class.php of the component Leave Request REST API. The manipulation leads to improper authorization. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. Upgrading to version 23.0.2 is recommended to address this issue. The identifier of the patch is ee93b6f2f9dd0f6aeefe9d718ab3ab0a44326b73. Upgrading the affected component is advised.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Dolibarr ERP CRM allows improper authorization in the Leave Request REST API, potentially enabling unauthorized users to access or manipulate sensitive data. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized actions, or privilege escalation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Dolibarr ERP CRM to version 23.0.2 or later to apply the security patch.
Review and monitor access logs for any unauthorized access attempts or suspicious activity.
Implement additional access control mechanisms, such as IP whitelisting or multi-factor authentication, to secure the Leave Request REST API.
